description: The shift in photographic sharing preference from Flickr (pro-enthusiast) to Instagram (mobile-casual) accelerated by Facebook's acquisition of Instagram in 2012. This tracks the pivot from desktop-centric to mobile-first social media.

key_events.description: Facebook acquires Instagram for $1 billion in April 2012, signaling dominance.
Flickr announces a major mobile app overhaul, but it fails to capture the casual market.
Instagram surpasses 100 million active users, cementing its status as the new photo-sharing standard.

calibration_notes: Models the transition between demographic segments (pro-photographers vs. general public mobile users).
